title: Fixing Node.js Express Middleware for Handling Nested Routes with Incorrect Route Resolution

statement.markdown: Write a Node.js Express middleware function that handles nested routes with a subtle logical bug. The function should correctly resolve the route based on the request URL and return the corresponding response. However, the current implementation has a bug that causes the function to return the wrong response for certain URLs.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an Express request object and a callback function to be called with the response.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the response from the callback function or throw an error.
statement.notes: The function should handle nested routes correctly and return the correct response for each URL.

constraints.description: The number of routes in the middleware function

examples.explanation: The function should return 'Hello from admin/users' for the given URL and method
The function should return 'Hello from admin/users/123' for the given URL and method

hints.text: Check the route resolution logic for nested routes
Consider using a recursive function to handle nested routes

starterCode.javascript: function nestedRoutes(req, res, next) { if (req.url.startsWith('/admin')) { // TO DO } next(); }

buggyStarterCode.javascript: function nestedRoutes(req, res, next) { if (req.url.startsWith('/admin')) { if (req.url.startsWith('/admin/users')) { res.send('Hello from admin/users'); } else if (req.url.startsWith('/admin/users/')) { res.send('Hello from admin/users/' + req.url.split('/admin/users/')[1]); } } else { next(); } }
